rules.rationale: Multiple null tokens for the same logical state (['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null'] (plus ['NONE', 'NULL', 'OO"', 'ZZZZ'] for this field)) confuses every downstream null-check and creates spurious duplicates on PK fields.
rules.action: Coerce ['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null', 'NONE', 'NULL', 'OO"', 'ZZZZ'] -> null

rules.rationale: Multiple null tokens for the same logical state (['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null']) confuses every downstream null-check and creates spurious duplicates on PK fields.
rules.action: Coerce ['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null'] -> null

rules.rationale: Multiple null tokens for the same logical state (['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null']) confuses every downstream null-check and creates spurious duplicates on PK fields.
rules.action: Coerce ['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null'] -> null

rules.rationale: Multiple null tokens for the same logical state (['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null']) confuses every downstream null-check and creates spurious duplicates on PK fields.
rules.action: Coerce ['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null'] -> null

rules.action: Coerce ['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null'] -> null

rules.rationale: Multiple null tokens for the same logical state (['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null']) confuses every downstream null-check and creates spurious duplicates on PK fields.
rules.action: Coerce ['', 'N/A', 'NA', 'NULL', 'NaN', 'NaT', 'None', 'n/a', 'nan', 'none', 'null'] -> null

rules.rationale: Three case-variants of each enum value present ('New Order' / 'NEW ORDER' / 'new order'). Any case-sensitive downstream join, group-by, or surveillance rule will treat them as distinct event types.
rules.action: Coerce to Title Case (canonical form).

rules.rationale: Values that don't match the canonical enum after case normalisation are almost certainly column-shift contamination (e.g., an ISIN sitting in the MessageType column).
rules.action: Flag rows where MessageType ∉ ['Cancel Order', 'Fill', 'New Order', 'Reject', 'Replace Order'] (no value change — Stage P2 will decide on recovery vs quarantine).

rules.rationale: ≥6 distinct date formats present in this column, including the ambiguous DD-MM vs MM-DD case. Free-form date strings break downstream date logic and surveillance windowing.
rules.action: Parse against an explicit format list ['%Y-%m-%d', '%d-%m-%Y', '%d-%m-%y', '%d/%m/%Y', '%m/%d/%Y', '%Y%m%d'] and re-emit as ISO YYYY-MM-DD. Per-format match counts: {"%Y-%m-%d": 234800, "%d-%m-%Y": 1591, "%d-%m-%y": 1564, "%d/%m/%Y": 2952, "%m/%d/%Y": 0, "%Y%m%d": 1522}

rules.rationale: Values that match NO known format (e.g., '0.0', '12.36') are column-shift artifacts where a Price value has shifted into the date column.
rules.action: Set unparsable values to null and flag the row.

rules.rationale: Timestamps must be ISO-8601 with millisecond precision and Z (UTC). Non-conforming values are column-shift artifacts (top examples: 'Sell', 'Buy', 'nan').
rules.action: Set non-ISO timestamps to null and flag the row.

rules.rationale: Primary keys must conform to the YYYY-MM-DD.RTnnn.P.nnn pattern. Values like '100', '400' (TotalVolume) and '1700' (TotalVolume) are column-shift contamination.
rules.action: Set malformed PK values to null and flag the row (the row remains in the dataset but is now correctly identified as having a missing PK).

rules.rationale: Primary keys cannot be null — a record with no PK cannot be deduplicated, reconciled to upstream OMS, or referenced by downstream Cancel/Fill events.
rules.action: Flag rows with empty MessageId (kept in dataset but will be excluded from PK-dependent metrics in Stage P3).

rules.rationale: Duplicate PKs imply either (a) replay-from-upstream, (b) merge artifacts from the malformed-quote contamination, or (c) seed-time duplication. Each requires a different remediation.
rules.action: Detected 17,671 rows participating in PK duplicates (covering 8,916 duplicate occurrences). Flagged but not modified at P1.

rules.rationale: ISIN is a 12-character ISO 6166 identifier (2-letter country + 9 alnum + check digit). Non-conforming values include 'SYS_OMEGA' (column shift from TransactionSource) and any sub-12-char strings.
rules.action: Set non-ISIN-shaped values to null and flag the row. NB: we validate format only, not the check-digit, in this stage.

rules.rationale: Side is a closed enum: ['Buy', 'Sell']. Observed contaminants include 'USD' (Currency) and 'SYS_OMEGA' (TransactionSource) — same shift signature as the other affected fields.
rules.action: Set non-{Buy,Sell} values to null and flag the row.

rules.rationale: Price must be a non-negative decimal. Non-parseable strings indicate contamination.
rules.action: Coerce to float; non-parseable -> null + flag.

rules.rationale: Negative prices are nonsensical for surveillance — a negative price cannot trigger any meaningful spoofing/layering/wash-trade rule.
rules.action: Set negative prices to null + flag.

rules.rationale: Volume must be a positive integer; zero or negative cannot produce a surveillance event.
rules.action: Coerce to numeric; non-parseable or ≤0 -> null + flag.

rules.rationale: Format check against ^[A-Z]{3,5}\d{1,3}\.[A-Z]+$. Non-matching values are likely contamination or schema drift; surfaced for review.
rules.action: Flag-only (no value change at P1).

rules.rationale: Format check against ^[A-Z]{3,5}\d{1,3}\.[A-Z]+$. Non-matching values are likely contamination or schema drift; surfaced for review.
rules.action: Flag-only (no value change at P1).

rules.rationale: Format check against ^TRD_\d{1,3}$. Non-matching values are likely contamination or schema drift; surfaced for review.
rules.action: Flag-only (no value change at P1).

rules.rationale: Format check against ^[A-Z]{2,6}$. Non-matching values are likely contamination or schema drift; surfaced for review.
rules.action: Flag-only (no value change at P1).

rules.rationale: A row that fails ≥2 independent format checks is almost certainly column-shifted (single random defects are unlikely to coincide). This is the population that Stage P2 will either re-align or quarantine.
rules.action: Compute COLUMN_SHIFT_SCORE = sum of ['FLAG_MT_UNKNOWN_ENUM', 'FLAG_DATE_UNPARSABLE', 'FLAG_TT_NOT_ISO', 'FLAG_PK_INVALID', 'FLAG_ISIN_INVALID', 'FLAG_SIDE_INVALID']. Rows with score≥2 = 0.
